response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map projects, and real distributed-systems experience from high-throughput microservices work at Dunnhumby. However, the role is explicitly C++-first, and his C++ usage is both minimal and last exercised over a decade ago at National Instruments (2014–2015). His entire career trajectory has consolidated around TypeScript and Node.js, with no Rust experience at all. He has no demonstrable background in routing data pipelines, tile generation, or navigation-guidance systems, which are the core deliverables of this position.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development via GISCollective and Green Map, demonstrating real GIS data collection and mapping workflows
High-throughput distributed-systems exposure at Dunnhumby, engineering low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong CS foundation from ETH Zürich and a long track record of shipping production software across varied domains
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old; no Rust experience at all — both primary languages for this role are effectively absent from his active skill set
No exposure to routing data pipelines, map-tile generation, or navigation-guidance systems, which are the core technical focus of the position
Career trajectory is firmly anchored in TypeScript/Node.js web and SaaS products, making a pivot to systems-level C++ geospatial routing work a very large gap
